query: Trump Taiwan arms package $14 billion notification Congress May 2026 status
answer:
I now have sufficient information to compile a comprehensive research report. Let me synthesize all findings.

---

## Research Report: Trump Taiwan Arms Package — "$14 Billion" Congressional Notification Status (May 2026)

### Executive Summary

The Trump administration notified Congress on **December 17, 2025** of approximately **$11.1 billion** in arms sales to Taiwan — the **largest single arms package for Taiwan in history**. This was formally processed through the Defense Security Cooperation Agency (DSCA). When combined with November 2025 sales (~$1.03 billion), the second-term cumulative total reaches approximately **$12.1 billion**, though the "$14 billion" figure is widely used in media (likely including additional pipeline items, training, and support costs). As of **May 2026**, Trump has stated he has **not yet made a final decision** on whether the package can proceed, describing Taiwan arms sales as a "very good negotiating chip" with China following his May 13–15, 2026 summit with Xi Jinping in Beijing.

---

### Key Findings

#### 1. The December 2025 Arms Package (DSCA Notification to Congress)
On **December 17, 2025**, the DSCA formally notified Congress of the following arms sales to Taiwan (TECRO):

| Item | Value (millions) |
|------|------:|
| ALTIUS-700M and ALTIUS-600 drone systems | $1,100 |
| Harpoon Missile Repair Follow-on Support | $91.4 |
| 1,050 Javelin Missile Systems | $375 |
| 1,545 TOW missile systems | $353 |
| 82 M142 HIMARS, 420 ATACMS, 756 GMLRS-U, 447 GMLRS-AW | $4,050 |
| 60 M109A7 Self-Propelled Howitzers, 60 M992A3 CAT vehicles, 13 M88A2 Recovery Vehicles, 4,080 PGK, 42 IFATDS | $4,030 |
| AH-1W Helicopter Spare and Repair Parts | $96 |
| Tactical Mission Network Software | $1,010 |
| **December 2025 Total** | **~$11,105M (~$11.1B)** |

This package was described as "geared toward countering an amphibious invasion of Taiwan" (Reuters, Nikkei Asia). Nikkei Asia described it as arming "Taiwan to block China's potential D-Day-style landing."

#### 2. Additional November 2025 Sales
- **November 13, 2025**: Non-standard spare and repair parts for F-16, C-130, and IDF aircraft — **$330 million**
- **November 18, 2025**: NASAMS air defense system — **$700 million** (confirmed by Reuters)

#### 3. The "$14 Billion" Figure
The "$14 billion" figure referenced in the Taipei Times (May 21, 2026: "Trump says he plans to speak with President Lai on arms sale") and other outlets likely represents the **cumulative value** of all second-term arms sales plus pipeline costs, training, and support contracts. The itemized DSCA notifications total ~$12.1 billion; the gap may be accounted for by additional support agreements, logistics, or items not individually broken out.

#### 4. Status as of May 2026: Trump "Weighing" the Package

**Key developments in May 2026:**

- **May 11, 2026**: Bloomberg reported Trump said he would **discuss Taiwan arms sales** with Xi Jinping at their upcoming Beijing summit, "potentially breaking with the Six Assurances policy" (which includes that the U.S. will not hold prior consultations with China on arms sales to Taiwan). Source: McBride, Sullivan, Lee — Bloomberg News.

- **May 13–15, 2026**: Trump-Xi Beijing Summit. Taiwan arms sales were a significant topic of discussion. Xi warned Trump that the Taiwan issue could lead to a US-China clash "if handled poorly." Trump described Taiwan arms sales as a **"very good negotiating chip"** in dealings with China (AP News).

- **Post-Summit (May 16–21, 2026)**:
  - **AP News headline**: "Trump weighs Taiwan arms package after summit aimed at steadying US-China ties" — Trump stated he has **"not yet made a decision"** on whether the major arms sale can move forward.
  - **AP News**: "Congressmen on both sides support arms sale to Taiwan despite Trump's hesitation" — bipartisan Congressional support for the sale continues.
  - **Taipei Times (May 21, 2026)**: "Trump says he plans to speak with President Lai on arms sale" — Trump indicated he would speak with President Lai Ching-te regarding the US$14 billion weapons sale, and stated "Well, I'll speak to him. I speak to everybody."
  - **President Lai Ching-te (May 20–21, 2026)**: Said he would tell Trump that arms purchases from the US are "essential for peace" and "crucial for regional stability." The Presidential Office reiterated Taiwan is a "sovereign, independent nation" after Trump said Taiwan should not "go independent."
  - **Taipei Times sidebar (May 22, 2026)**: "Trump weighs Taiwan arms package" appears as a trending/most popular story.

#### 5. Congressional Stance
- **Bipartisan support** in Congress for the arms sale to proceed despite Trump's ambivalence (AP News).
- Congress was formally notified of the arms sales through DSCA in **November–December 2025**. Under the Arms Export Control Act, Congress typically has a **30-day review period** after notification, during which it can block a sale via a joint resolution. No blocking action has been reported.
- In **March 2026**, Taiwan's parliament authorized the government to sign stalled U.S. arms deals (The Straits Times, March 13, 2026).

#### 6. Broader Context — Other 2026 Defense Cooperation
- **January 2026**: US and Taiwan agreed to jointly produce 155mm caliber shells.
- **February 2026**: NCSIST and Kratos Defense tested a jointly-developed jet-powered attack drone.
- **March 2026**: Taiwan began receiving MQ-9B SkyGuardian drones from prior purchases.
- **April 2026**: US-Taiwan joint initiative to manufacture 120×570mm NATO ammunition.
- **May 2026**: HIMARS to be deployed to Taiwan's outlying islands of Penghu and Dongyin.

#### 7. Diplomatic Climate
- The post-summit atmosphere was conciliatory, with a **"tentative tariff cut agreement"** and $17B in trade deals (Boeing jets, beef, soybeans, rare earths).
- US Navy Taiwan Strait transits have been reduced under Trump's second term — only **1 confirmed transit in 2026** (January 16–17), versus ~13/year at peak.
- Secretary Rubio stated Taiwan "did not feature prominently" in summit discussions, though this seems contradicted by the intensity of post-summit coverage.

---

### Bottom Line on Status

The December 2025 **Congressional notification has been completed** through the DSCA process. The formal 30-day Congressional review period has **long since passed** without blocking action. However, **final executive approval/contract execution** is now in question. Trump has indicated he is using the arms package as leverage in broader US-China negotiations. As of May 21–22, 2026, the sale's **final disposition remains uncertain**, with Trump describing it as under active consideration and indicating he will speak with both Xi and Lai about it.

---

key_facts: The Trump administration notified Congress on December 17, 2025 of approximately $11.1 billion in arms sales to Taiwan through the DSCA — the largest single Taiwan arms package in history.

The December 2025 package includes 82 HIMARS ($4.05B), 60 M109A7 howitzers ($4.03B), ALTIUS drones ($1.1B), Tactical Mission Network Software ($1.01B), 1,050 Javelins ($375M), 1,545 TOW missiles ($353M), Harpoon repair support ($91.4M), and AH-1W helicopter parts ($96M).

Additional November 2025 sales included NASAMS air defense ($700M) and F-16/C-130/IDF spare parts ($330M), bringing the Trump second-term cumulative total to approximately $12.1 billion in itemized DSCA notifications.

The '$14 billion' figure widely cited in media (including Taipei Times, May 21, 2026) likely includes pipeline items, training, support contracts, and logistics beyond the itemized DSCA notifications.

On May 11, 2026, Bloomberg reported Trump said he would discuss Taiwan arms sales at the Xi summit, potentially breaking with the Six Assurances policy (which includes no prior consultation with China on Taiwan arms sales).
At the Trump-Xi Beijing summit (May 13-15, 2026), Xi warned Taiwan could lead to a US-China clash 'if handled poorly.' Trump described Taiwan arms sales as a 'very good negotiating chip' with China.
As of May 21-22, 2026, Trump has stated he has 'not yet made a decision' on whether the major arms sale can move forward, per AP News.
Trump indicated he would speak with Taiwan President Lai Ching-te about the arms sale, saying 'Well, I'll speak to him. I speak to everybody' (Taipei Times, May 21, 2026).
Bipartisan Congressional support for the arms sale continues — 'Congressmen on both sides support arms sale to Taiwan despite Trump's hesitation' (AP News).
The Congressional notification through DSCA was completed in November-December 2025, and the 30-day Congressional review period passed without blocking action.
Taiwan President Lai Ching-te stated that if given a chance to speak with Trump, he would tell him arms purchases from the US are 'essential for peace' and 'crucial for regional stability' (AP News).
In March 2026, Taiwan's parliament authorized the government to sign stalled US arms deals (The Straits Times).
HIMARS deployment to Taiwan's outlying islands of Penghu and Dongyin was announced in May 2026 (Taipei Times, May 11, 2026).
US Navy Taiwan Strait transits have been at reduced pace under Trump's second term — only 1 confirmed transit in 2026 (January 16-17) vs ~13/year at peak in 2020.
